content:
# Content Voice and Tone Preferences — Lim Chun Zhe (2026-03-29)

Learned during the 27-day reflection post collaboration. These preferences apply to all future LinkedIn posts, carousels, and public-facing content.

## Tone and Framing

- **Humble, not arrogant.** Never frame things as "I didn't expect this to work so well." Instead: "I didn't expect it to be ready yet." The surprise is at the tool, not at the reader.
- **Honest about imperfections.** Not everything ships perfectly. Still learning AI-assisted development. Say it directly. Readers respect authenticity over polish.
- **Let it breathe.** Don't condense so much that it feels rushed. If a beat deserves 3 sentences, give it 3 sentences. Longer is fine for reflection pieces.
- **Context before examples.** Don't drop specific scenarios (like "auth system redesigned across three repos") without first explaining what category of thing it is. Set up the concept, then give the concrete example.
- **Jokes land as jokes, not complaints.** "When they ran the 2x token promotion, I was out diving and missed most of it. Timing. hahaha" — light, self-deprecating. Never frame constraints as grievances.
- **"hahaha" is acceptable.** Casual laughter is part of the voice. Don't sanitize it.

## Perspective and Identity

- **Always intended as a product.** corvia was never "just a portfolio project that accidentally became real." It was always intended as a real product. The portfolio aspect is the job-search context, not the identity.
- **Portable development tool.** A core motivation: owning your own dev tool that travels with you across roles and projects. Every role change = wasted time rebuilding env. corvia solves that.
- **Job seeker context.** Currently looking for next role. This grounds the story. Open and close with it when appropriate. Not ashamed. #OpenToWork.
- **Left previous role, started immediately.** Friday was last day, started building that weekend. Don't over-emphasize but it shows drive.
- **Learning GenAI firsthand.** Started corvia partly to understand generative AI by building in it, not just reading about it. Hands-on >> blog posts. This is a core belief.

## What NOT to frame as selling points

- **Zero-dependency is not a theme.** The user loves Docker. The devcontainer uses Docker. Don't frame "no Docker needed" as a win.
- **"Portfolio to product" is not the arc.** Always intended as a product from day one.
- **Speed/velocity is not the main story.** 382 commits in 27 days is impressive but it's context, not the headline.

## What to highlight

- **Dogfooding pride.** Using corvia to build corvia is the emotional core. But be honest: it wasn't available day 1. There was a bootstrap phase with markdown files.
- **The loop evolved gradually.** Day 1 was brainstorm + design + build. The 5-persona review, session history, agent coordination came later. Some early decisions got missed. The process and product co-evolved.
- **Grabbing emerging capabilities.** When Claude ships Agent Teams, immediately build an adapter. When GitNexus appears, start building structural intelligence. The mindset: AI moves fast, grab every capability and build on it. That's how you stay on edge.
- **Claude convergence = exciting.** When major players build in the same direction, it validates the problem. Not threatening. Complementary. Personal memory is table stakes. Organizational layer is still open.
- **Real-world validation.** Interview take-home finished in <24 hours using corvia methodology. First test outside corvia's own development. The methodology transferred.
- **Features not fully internalized yet.** Many recent capabilities are built and tested but not fully used. It takes time as a human to understand how tools change your workflow. This is an honest, relatable insight.
- **Claude Max 5 constraint.** Not cheap when unemployed, but committed. Play it light, not as a complaint.

## Style Rules

- No em dashes. Use periods and short sentences.
- No "surprised me" hooks. Prefer "interesting" for discoveries.
- Show-don't-tell with specific numbers and concrete details.
- End with an engagement question.
- Reference carousel slides in the post text.
- Carousel feature showcases should be clean and impactful, not cluttered. Group capabilities into pillars with taglines.
